extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won stage 1 via perfectly timed sprint launch at high speed, holding off EWAN Caleb before Ewan could come around.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma delivered complete leadout for non-pure sprinter Van Aert, revealing willingness to commit full train for stage-hunting GC rider.
Quick Step isolated Ballerini with no leadout riders despite having numbers, suggesting team coordination breakdown or pre-race tactical miscalculation.
UAE Emirates positioned Gaviria perfectly on left side but lost on raw power, confirming positioning alone insufficient against stronger finishers.
Van Aert initiated sprint early at high speed rather than waiting, exploiting reduced pure sprinter acceleration advantage at extreme velocity.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Won bunch sprint despite not being pure sprinter, took 10-second GC bonus unexpectedly early, showing stage race ambition
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: neutral: Positioned well on Van Aert wheel but couldn't come around despite pure sprinter credentials, suggesting form uncertainty
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: negative: Quick Step left him without leadout support despite team numbers, finished only 8th in bunch sprint
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: UAE gave perfect positioning but lacked final punch for podium, raising questions about current top-end speed
